title: Refactor config loading to support per-vendor config files
body:
## Summary

Refactor `load_config()` and `save_config()` across all three framework commands (install, check, remove) to support per-vendor config files under `.vendored/configs/`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `load_config()` scans `.vendored/configs/` for `<vendor>.json` files
- [ ] Vendor name is derived from filename (e.g., `pearls.json` → `pearls`)
- [ ] Each `<vendor>.json` contains vendor settings directly (no wrapping `vendors` key)
- [ ] `save_config()` in templates/install writes individual `configs/<vendor>.json`
- [ ] `save_config()` in templates/remove deletes `configs/<vendor>.json` when removing a vendor
- [ ] Backwards compat: falls back to monolithic `config.json` if `configs/` dir doesn't exist or has no `.json` files
- [ ] `protected` field preserved in per-vendor config for v1 fallback compatibility
- [ ] Tests updated in test_install.py, test_check.py, test_remove.py

## Key Files

- `templates/install` — `load_config()` at L39, `save_config()` at L48
- `templates/check` — `load_config()` at L31
- `templates/remove` — `load_config()` at L26, `save_config()` at L35

## Per-vendor Config Schema

```json
{
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "install_branch": "chore/install-<name>",
  "private": false,
  "dogfood": false,
  "automerge": false,
  "protected": ["..."],
  "allowed": ["..."]
}
```

## Notes

- `config.json` remains on disk (for framework-level settings and migration detection)
- The `vendors` key in `config.json` is only used during backwards-compat fallback
- Return format from `load_config()` stays the same: `{"vendors": {"name": {...}, ...}}`

title: Pass VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR env var to vendor install.sh
body:
## Summary

Pass `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` environment variable to vendor `install.sh` scripts so vendors can install files under `.vendored/pkg/<vendor>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `download_and_run_install()` sets `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR=.vendored/pkg/<vendor>` in run_env
- [ ] Framework creates `.vendored/pkg/<vendor>/` directory before running install.sh
- [ ] Dogfood exception: does NOT set `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` when vendor has `"dogfood": true`
- [ ] `templates/remove` deletes `.vendored/pkg/<vendor>/` directory when removing a vendor
- [ ] `cleanup_empty_dirs()` handles `pkg/` parent directory cleanup
- [ ] Tests: env var set correctly, directory created, dogfood exception, removal cleans up

## Key Files

- `templates/install` — `download_and_run_install()` at L241, env vars at L280-285
- `templates/remove` — `get_files_to_remove()` at L51, `cleanup_empty_dirs()` at L83

## Contract

Vendors SHOULD install files under `$VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` but MAY install to other paths (workflows, hooks) when the target system requires specific locations. All installed files must appear in the manifest regardless of location.

If `$VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` is not set, vendor falls back to its original layout (backwards compat).

## Notes

- Manifest-based protection in `check` automatically covers pkg/ paths — no check changes needed
- Stale file cleanup on migration (old dotdir → pkg/) is out of scope — consumers use `remove` + `install` to migrate

title: Write vendor compliance guide for directory restructure
body:
## Summary

Write a markdown implementation guide that lives in the repo and instructs vendor repos (and AI tools working on them) on how to comply with the new directory structure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Markdown file at `docs/vendor-install-dir-guide.md`
- [ ] Documents `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` contract: env var, when it's set, expected behavior
- [ ] Documents per-vendor config schema (fields, types, defaults)
- [ ] Shows before/after examples of vendor file layouts
- [ ] Provides code examples: how a vendor's `install.sh` reads and uses `$VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR`
- [ ] Documents fallback behavior when `$VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` is not set
- [ ] Lists what vendor repos need to change
- [ ] Documents migration path for consumer repos (remove + re-install)

## Audience

AI agents and developers working on vendor repos (pearls, git-dogfood, git-semver) who need to update their `install.sh` scripts for compatibility with the new directory structure.

## Notes

- Vendor repo changes are out of scope for this epic — only the guide is in scope
- The guide should be actionable: someone reading it should know exactly what to change

title: Update README and docs for new directory layout
body:
## Summary

Update the README and existing documentation to reflect the new `.vendored/` directory layout after all implementation tasks are complete.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README documents new `.vendored/` layout (configs/, pkg/, manifests/)
- [ ] README documents `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` env var in contract/env vars section
- [ ] README documents per-vendor config format and schema
- [ ] Migration notes section for existing consumer repos
- [ ] Remove references to per-vendor dotdirs at repo root as the primary layout

## Key Files

- `README.md`
- Any other docs referencing the old layout

title: Add config migration from monolithic config.json to per-vendor configs/
body:
## Summary

Add a migration function that splits monolithic `config.json` vendors into individual `configs/<vendor>.json` files. Triggered automatically during install when monolithic config is detected.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `migrate_config()` function reads `config.json` `vendors` dict, writes each vendor to `configs/<vendor>.json`
- [ ] Creates `.vendored/configs/` directory if it doesn't exist
- [ ] Removes `vendors` key from `config.json` after successful migration (file remains for framework-level use)
- [ ] Called from `templates/install` main() when: monolithic config has `vendors` AND `configs/` dir has no `.json` files
- [ ] Idempotent: running migration twice doesn't duplicate or corrupt data
- [ ] Logs migration action to stderr for visibility
- [ ] Tests: creates correct files, cleans up config.json, idempotency

## Key Files

- `templates/install` — add `migrate_config()`, call from `main()` before `load_config()`

## Migration Trigger Logic

```python
# In main(), before load_config():
if os.path.exists(CONFIG_PATH):
    with open(CONFIG_PATH) as f:
        raw = json.load(f)
    if "vendors" in raw and not any(f.endswith('.json') for f in os.listdir(CONFIGS_DIR)):
        migrate_config(raw)
```

title: Update bootstrap install.sh for new directory structure
body:
## Summary

Update the bootstrap `install.sh` to create the new `configs/` and `pkg/` directories during framework installation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `install.sh` creates `.vendored/configs/` directory during bootstrap
- [ ] `install.sh` creates `.vendored/pkg/` directory during bootstrap
- [ ] Existing `mkdir -p` line updated to include new directories
- [ ] Config migration is NOT done in install.sh — it runs when `templates/install` is invoked (keeps bash simple, migration logic in Python)
- [ ] Tests in test_installer.py verify new directory creation

## Key Files

- `install.sh` — directory creation at L49

## Notes

- `install.sh` is a bash script that must stay simple — it just creates dirs and downloads files
- The actual config migration runs when `templates/install` is first invoked after the framework update
- This is blocked by Issues 1 and 2 because the directories only make sense once the commands know how to use them

title: install_new_vendor post-validate broken when per-vendor configs active
